items.title: STAT 534: Statistical Computing
items.source_text: Introduction to scientific computing. Programming tools, modern programming methodologies (modularization, object oriented design), design of data structures and algorithms, numerical computing and graphics. Uses C++ for several substantial scientific programming projects.

items.title: BIOST 518: Applied Biostatistics II
items.source_text: Multiple regression for continuous, discrete, and right-censored response variables, including dummy variables, transformations, and interactions. Introduction to regression with correlated outcome data, model and case diagnostics, and computer assignments using real data and standard statistical computer packages.

items.title: URBDP 520: Quantitative Methods in Urban Design and Planning
items.source_text: Methods of statistical and mathematical analysis in design and planning, emphasizing the use of computer packages for analyzing urban data, including regression, matrix methods, and cohort-survival population models. The course establishes a solid fact basis for planning activities and decision-making, covering statistical analysis and techniques, demographic techniques, geographical and spatial analysis, and economic analysis.

items.title: ENVIR 495: Climate Justice
topics: systems of oppression in environmental policy
items.source_text: This course explores the concept of climate justice — including its histories, diversities, and crucial importance for re-imagining our planet and relations. Climate crises are long-standing, differentiated, and experienced through compounding systems of settler-colonialism, racism, class and casteism, and patriarchy. Solutions that fail to address systems of oppression will not achieve climate justice.

items.title: ECON 411: Behavioral Economics
items.source_text: The course incorporates insights from psychology into economic models and examines evidence suggesting individuals systematically depart from traditional economic assumptions. It modifies these assumptions to construct models that generate sharp and testable predictions. Students learn how to incorporate behavioral ideas to shape markets and policy, with readings including Thaler and Sunstein's Nudge.

items.title: EDPSY 591: Methods of Educational Research
items.source_text: Focus on experimental and quasi-experimental research design methods, as well as APA writing standards. Recommended for beginning graduate students who need a broad overview of educational research methods. Includes discussions applying and critically evaluating research methods in social science research contexts.
